[2018-06-08 11:19] zakisan1 (Zakisan): I'm wondering why in a game with Xaetron and a 5 health absorber (e.g. Energy Matrix) it is better to absorb on Xaetron every second turn instead of absorbing on Matrix every turn and clicking Xaetron (assuming that you are already past the absorb barrier):
Option A: Alternate absorbing: You get an average of (4+8)/2=6 absorb
Option B: Only absorb on Matrix, click Xaetron when possible: 4 absorb + 5*(4/7) damage= 4absorb +2,86 damage. 

We have to inflate the absorb by half a turn to compare with damage, so it suggests A is only better when half-turn inflation is >2,86/2=1,43, right? Or does the picture change when you account for inflation in atlernating the blocking?
[2018-06-08 11:21] leary93: It is not better to absorb on xaetron with e-matrix in the set
[2018-06-08 11:22] leary93: its better to always click, as that gets more damage differential out
[2018-06-08 11:22] leary93: Even with xeno, it's already arguable that you dont absorb on xae
[2018-06-08 11:22] leary93: but at least you would be alternating if you are allowed to
[2018-06-08 11:23] leary93: arguably tho, you wouldnt really get xaetron in an e-matrix set, unless green > blue
[2018-06-08 11:24] zakisan1 (Zakisan): I was watching a game with a Xaetron, Matrix, Synth and Gauss Fab, where it seemed reasonable to get both: 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9NQB4ZGbc0U&list=PLKDpSfFMS1VTUDDYA0t9qWWIMTfM_S3ku&index=2
WDIL: [NativeAvian] vs KP - YouTube
Replay code: msitH-2e+NA
[2018-06-08 11:25] leary93: I think it's a mistake to absorb on xaetron tho, you'd click it if you get it
[2018-06-08 11:26] leary93: guass makes xae reasonable to get
[2018-06-08 11:27] leary93: so thats fine, but consider this right: alternate absorbing makes xae give +1 bv a turn, while clicking it while you can gives it ~2.85 bv a turn
[2018-06-08 11:27] zakisan1 (Zakisan): Okay, that makes sense.
[2018-06-08 11:27] leary93: if you absorb once every 3 turns it gives 2.33~ bv a turn
[2018-06-08 11:28] zakisan1 (Zakisan): I guess with only wall as regular absorb that's different?
[2018-06-08 11:28] leary93: with wall you sometimes might still alternate tho
[2018-06-08 11:28] leary93: because xae pretty much just adds absorb on top if it can regen for free
[2018-06-08 11:29] leary93: but at least not alternating isnt bad
[2018-06-08 11:29] leary93: Like, whenever I play Xae I always look to get free recovery off
[2018-06-08 11:29] leary93: because that's the strenght of Xae
[2018-06-08 11:30] leary93: If you dont alternate vs wall, you get ~+2 bv absorb, if you alternate ever 2 turns then you get ~+3 bv absorb, if you alternate every 3 turns then you get 3.33 bv absorb
[2018-06-08 11:30] leary93: for which the 3 turn alternate is prob horrible when your other best asborb is wall
[2018-06-08 11:30] zakisan1 (Zakisan): I'll try to do that as well 😉 Thanks!
[2018-06-08 11:30] leary93: because +.33 bv isnt worth the inflation I think
[2018-06-08 11:31] zakisan1 (Zakisan): yeah, and you have to overdefend *more* as well, so probably alternate

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-06-11) ---
[2018-06-11 08:14] uchinanchu: Hi guys, just picked up Prismata and learning the basics; what are the BASIC mistakes I made in this replay?
[2018-06-11 08:15] uchinanchu: I was p1 against what seems like an obvious fastimus p2; I realized early that my position was not great, so I'm sure I made a lot of mistakes later too, but I'd like to hear about the opening phase more. Thanks!
[2018-06-11 08:16] namington: I'm not sure I understand the t4 conduit
[2018-06-11 08:17] namington: like yeah, it gets forcefields except thats super inefficient soak early on
[2018-06-11 08:17] namington: especially factoring in the 4 noninflationary gold invested early
[2018-06-11 08:19] namington: the most efficient units in the set are lancetooth, grimbotch, and caut - it's possible the set's tactical enough that lancetooths dont get bought, but I'm still pretty sure you always blastforge t6 in response to his forge
[2018-06-11 08:19] namington: that a) allows you to lancetooth against walls, getting you absorb denial and b) sets up for cauterizers if that doesnt happen
[2018-06-11 08:19] extratricky: the most basic thing that you should focus on is that you want to absorb as much as possible.
[2018-06-11 08:20] extratricky: for example, on turn 4, buying a rhino instead of a grimbotch lets you absorb for 1, and would have been better
[2018-06-11 08:20] namington: also, attacking with 1 lifespan grimbotches when you're taking damage
[2018-06-11 08:20] namington: is very rarely correct
[2018-06-11 08:20] namington: and by "very rarely" I mean "basically never unless youre breaching or exploiting"
[2018-06-11 08:20] namington: "or can be exploited"
[2018-06-11 08:20] extratricky: on turn 10 you lose forcefield and rhino when you could have lost forcefield and engineer instead
[2018-06-11 08:21] namington: think of it like this: attacking with them is 1 damage, and defending is 2 damage worth of soak (well, 2/r damage worth of soak, but you probably dont know what that means)
[2018-06-11 08:22] namington: the thing is, in theory he can lancetooth to deny your 1 lifespan botches, but allowing that is still better than doing an inefficient play like forcefielding
[2018-06-11 08:22] extratricky: There were a lot of mistakes caused by the interaction of grimbotch and lancetooth but I don't think you should focus on those yet
[2018-06-11 08:23] uchinanchu: I have to review it myself too with the context you folks are providing (thank you!), but I was just frustrated after maybe turn 7 tbh
[2018-06-11 08:23] extratricky: oh turn 6 is another turn where you denied yoruself absorb. Buy rhino instead of the grimbotch and then you don't need the forcefield
[2018-06-11 08:23] namington: honestly though the main thing I saw is underestimating rhino absorb and going for useless green
[2018-06-11 08:24] namington: when sets are very aggressive and dont have much efficient defense, we call them "tactical" - these are sets which put a lot of emphasis on one or two health exchanges
[2018-06-11 08:24] namington: while rhino absorb is quite low, in these matchups absoring on rhino saves you 1 health a turn
[2018-06-11 08:24] namington: which translates to a little over 2 gold (engineer cost, factoring in that 2 gold on an earlier turn > 2 gold later)
[2018-06-11 08:25] jugornot (Jug Or Not 👺): i have hard time reading sets with base+8
[2018-06-11 08:26] namington: meanwhile, the conduit bought you, like, two forcefields before turn 8 - that's 6 gold and 2 drones worth of investment for something that couldve been done for 8 gold
[2018-06-11 08:26] uchinanchu: this level of analysis is fascinating
[2018-06-11 08:26] uchinanchu: the conduit comment makes sense, and I wouldn't have thought to put it in context that way, but you kind of need to do that to evaluate it
[2018-06-11 08:26] uchinanchu: everything makes sense btw haha
[2018-06-11 08:26] uchinanchu: the other comments too, I mean
[2018-06-11 08:26] namington: honestly that set in a perfect world is probably low-econ monored, if one player buys forge the other mirrors for lancetooths
[2018-06-11 08:27] namington: ideally you want lancetooths in the set cause theyre good vs botches but
[2018-06-11 08:27] namington: its so hard to get them out on such a low econ
[2018-06-11 08:27] namington: lancetooths are kinda weird like that - they work best in higher econ sets, but by existing they make sets lower econ
[2018-06-11 08:27] namington: so they force weird situations sometimes
[2018-06-11 08:28] uchinanchu: honestly, I didn't even factor lancetooths into the plan (and this is just +5 set)... its hard for me to evaluate a lot of interactions, and the analysis provided just now is the best source I've found to improve tbh
[2018-06-11 08:29] uchinanchu: well, the analysis seems great but its up to me to take it in, so well see lol
[2018-06-11 08:29] zakisan1 (Zakisan): What do you think about animus t2 for p1? It's nice to be the first to pressure  but you cannot afford Rhino+Grimboth at turn 4 which feels bad. (1. DD 2. A  3. Grimbotch, Grimboth 4. RD (?) )
[2018-06-11 08:29] namington: I think elyot animus loses to cauts
[2018-06-11 08:29] uchinanchu: I thought about the elyot animus but I felt like it was too difficult for me to discern how to proceed
[2018-06-11 08:29] namington: hold on ill analyze it real quick
[2018-06-11 08:30] extratricky: if you do that then turn 3 should probably be D4instead of 33
[2018-06-11 08:30] extratricky: maybe it can do okay
[2018-06-11 08:31] extratricky: I wouldn't recommend trying elyot animus to a new player though
[2018-06-11 08:31] extratricky: it makes the game extremely difficult for you
[2018-06-11 08:31] namington: yeah, just analyzed it, DD A D4 seems to lose pretty hard
[2018-06-11 08:31] namington: DD A 33 has a better chance as it denies cauts from coming out but
[2018-06-11 08:32] namington: I'm pretty sure that still loses
[2018-06-11 08:33] namington: hm, there is a p1 line that denies cauts after t3 33 actually but
[2018-06-11 08:33] namington: its behind 3 drones all game
[2018-06-11 08:34] namington: so it loses as soon as botches run out of steam

[2018-06-12 03:40] uchinanchu: hello chat, beginner here; O0oAc-28Zh7
[2018-06-12 03:40] uchinanchu: can I get some commentary on my basic mistakes, and also some commentary on the interplay of the econ units in this set? thank you!
[2018-06-12 03:41] uchinanchu: I ended up winning, but its fascinating to see the high level analysis this chat provides, and the basic mistakes are important to see
[2018-06-12 03:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): u probs should get more econ than that
[2018-06-12 03:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): also rhinos suck and arka is brilliant
[2018-06-12 03:51] uchinanchu: I noted arka, but I felt like the random set dictated a low econ red line of play; that said, growing the econ and teching the blue for arka would have been correct here?
[2018-06-12 03:51] uchinanchu: oh. it is definitely a huge absorber, didn't think about that
[2018-06-12 03:51] namington: boreholes more efficient than tarsier in this set
[2018-06-12 03:51] camzace: yeah boreholes are great
[2018-06-12 03:51] namington: and in general wall is the biggest absorber in the set besides arka
[2018-06-12 03:52] namington: so you kinda want blue anyway
[2018-06-12 03:52] namington: and 2 blue for the best unit in the game isnt that much to ask
[2018-06-12 03:53] namington: also an aside: vivid drone was better than drone t2
[2018-06-12 03:53] namington: like, just straight-up better
[2018-06-12 03:53] namington: unless youre  worried about getting breached, but youre not.
[2018-06-12 03:53] uchinanchu: hmm... so would you say a 3 engi opening is right? or is this a grow econ gradually until arka approach
[2018-06-12 03:53] namington: your opening line was fine, I just woulda picked up blastforge at some point
[2018-06-12 03:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): just spam wilds into vivids
[2018-06-12 03:54] uchinanchu: thank you guys! I'll explore the line with the same set tonight and get the feel of what you guys are saying
[2018-06-12 03:55] namington: basically arka is stupidly efficient
[2018-06-12 03:55] namington: like, its absurdly good for its cost
[2018-06-12 03:55] namington: the "downside" with arka is that second arka counters first arka, if attack isnt 14 or more
[2018-06-12 03:56] namington: say you have 8 attack and buy arka and deal 1 damage
[2018-06-12 03:56] namington: your opponent also has 8 attack and buys arka, now they deal 1 damage into your arka (so 0 damage)
[2018-06-12 03:56] namington: you just spent 7 whole attack on your arka
[2018-06-12 03:56] namington: but your opponent would've had 6 attack get absorbed anyway
[2018-06-12 03:56] namington: so they didnt actually end up spending much attack
[2018-06-12 03:56] namington: you spent 7, they spent 2
[2018-06-12 03:57] namington: then, going into your turn, your opponent's arka still has prompt
[2018-06-12 03:57] namington: so basically, second arka denies almost an entire turn of arka absorb if attack isnt that high yet
[2018-06-12 03:57] namington: of course, if your opponent physically cant buy arka (not enough attack or tech) that doesnt matter
[2018-06-12 03:57] namington: in which case, arka is just stupidly efficient and you wanna buy it as soon as feasible
[2018-06-12 03:59] uchinanchu: 2nd arka denies the 1st arka's prompt absorb, by taking the attack that would've been absorbed into the arka investment
[2018-06-12 03:59] uchinanchu: I see, 2nd counters 1st, but obviously doesn't matter if other player can't get it
[2018-06-12 04:00] namington: yeah thats the fundamental idea
[2018-06-12 04:00] namington: it also doesnt matter much if both players have high enough attack that they're getting past arka anyway, even if they buy arka
[2018-06-12 04:00] namington: like if you have 14 attack and your opponent has an arka, your own arka is still denying you 7 damage
[2018-06-12 04:01] namington: as 6 would've got absorbed, and 8 wouldve got past - but if you buy arka, only 1 gets past
[2018-06-12 04:01] namington: (buying arka is still generally correct in those circumstances tho just cause its such a good unit, but its not AS good)
[2018-06-12 04:02] namington: as compared to if you have, say, 10 attack - 6 would've got absorbed and 4 would've got past, but if you buy arka, 0 gets past
[2018-06-12 04:02] namington: in the former situation, you lose 7 damage by buying arka
[2018-06-12 04:02] namington: in the latter situation, you only lose 4
[2018-06-12 04:02] namington: as the rest gets absorbed
[2018-06-12 04:04] uchinanchu: ah ok ok I had to go over that a couple times
[2018-06-12 04:04] uchinanchu: that all makes sense, this is a ton of help thanks!

[2018-06-12 19:52] xujhan: Centrifuge isn't particularly efficient, so it's really only worth going for if there are high impact units that you really want to rush but can't support the tech for. Vai and Odin both qualify if the set doesn't have good red/blue sinks, but Luci makes it really easy to spend RRRR so fuge doesn't benefit her as much as the other two. A single Luci also doesn't give as much immediate threat as either Odin or Vai (and is way, way more breach vulnerable) which reduces the incentive to rush her with a T2 Fuge.

Endo kit is really only worth getting if inflation is low, you want to be in those colours anyway, and either chill is unusually good (lifespan defenders and/or opp very breach vulnerable) or soak is unusually bad. 

Thorium gives such efficient openers that I don't think I've ever made fewer than 3. That said I've never had a set that was something like Thorium and 7 red rush units, so I imagine that in very fast sets you might want fewer.

Standard disclaimer that I'm bad and all of the above is probably wrong.
